title: Early Bengal identities enter the record
summary: Public records show Bengal Tigers and Bengals as part of the naming trail that should be preserved instead of flattened.
body: This part of the timeline acts like a memory anchor. Even before full scorecards are imported, preserving alias teams matters because community cricket histories often live across naming shifts, roster changes, and partial archives.
relatedStats: Alias tracking enabled for future imports

title: St Pete Shields adds a composed leather-ball chapter
summary: 7 innings, 3 not outs, and a strike rate above 122 suggest flexible batting responsibility.
body: This is a quieter season on the surface, but it has the texture of a useful role player year: staying unbeaten, keeping tempo high, and contributing across a smaller set of innings.

title: SWAT bowling spike arrives in the fall series
summary: 7 wickets in 8 matches, including a 4/30, adds a clear bowling chapter to the archive.
body: The all-rounder label becomes more believable when the timeline shows impact in different formats. The fall series gives the archive exactly that: a compact stretch where bowling takes center stage.

title: SWAT season turns into a batting-volume chapter
summary: 228 runs across Titan Cup XI makes SWAT one of the defining phases in the archive.
body: A season total often tells a better story than a single score. Here, the archive shows repeated contribution over nine matches and a 54 that can later be expanded into its own scorecard-linked memory.

title: TB Bengal Tigers becomes the current badge
summary: The most recent team chapter arrives with the best-known score in the current public dataset: 63.
body: This is where the archive feels most live. The current team is tied to strong recent batting numbers, a clearly named role, and room for richer match-by-match importing from here forward.

title: Allies match becomes a fielding memory
summary: Not every meaningful entry is a fifty or a four-for. Two catches can still hold the day together.
body: The recent Allies match is exactly why the section lives inside Adventures instead of Projects. Cricket is not just outputs — it is memory, alertness, teammates, and the details that stay long after a small score disappears from conversation.

title: The personal cricket archive is launched
summary: Data, memory, and story finally get a durable home under Adventures.
body: This site section begins as a curated version-one archive: public stats, seeded milestones, placeholders for future scorecards, and a structure ready for deeper imports. The point is not just completeness — it is making the cricket journey readable, expandable, and personal.
